question: Are off-label medications for children dangerous because they lack FDA approval?
topic: Corrects misconception about off-label prescribing that affects pediatric treatment acceptance

sources.name: Challenges for the FDA: The Future of Drug Safety: Workshop Summary

discovery_sources.text: Approximately 40% or higher of treatments for children with complex diseases are off-label
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: Kid Tested, FDA Approved: The Science of Pediatric Clinical Trials

discovery_sources.text: Kids.Dot trial took 20 years from conception to publication and guideline change
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: Kid Tested, FDA Approved: The Science of Pediatric Clinical Trials

discovery_sources.text: Pharmaceutical anticoagulant trials in children typically take close to a decade to complete, versus more rapid completion in adults
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: Kid Tested, FDA Approved: The Science of Pediatric Clinical Trials
